titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 22

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=106 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:27:59.663979+00 | translation moa=107 status=live modified=2016-06-07 10:55:21.323394+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filijs  Ricardus dei gratia episcopus sancti ANDREE apostoli . salutem ⁊ episcopalem benedictionem . Sciant tam posteri quam presentes . nos
dedisse ⁊ concessisse ⁊ presentis scripti pagina confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie beati ANDREE apostoli ⁊ canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus ⁊ seruituris
capellam de Inche Ђore cum omnibus eidem capellę iuste pertinentibus inperpetuam elemosinam pro salute anime méé ⁊ predecessorum ⁊ successorum meorum
animabus  saluis consuetudinibus episcopalibus . Testibus his . Gregorio episcopo dunkeldensi . Matheo archidiacono . de sancto Andrea . Magistro andrea archidiacono Laodon’.
Gaufrido abbate dumf’ . Aluredo abbate Striuelin Willelmo abbate de Sancta Cruce . Magistro herberto . Magistro Abraham . Alexandro capellano . Roberto filio
Seulfi de pert . Stephano clerico . Odone fratre Archidiaconi mathei .

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Richard by the grace of God bishop of St Andrew the apostle, greeting and episcopal benediction:

Let those to come as well as those present know that we have given and granted and by the present written document made firm, to God and to the church of blessed Andrew the apostle and to the canons serving God in that place now and in the future, the chapel of Inchture, with everything rightfully pertaining to the same chapel, in perpetual alms, for the welfare of my soul, and of my predecessors’ and successors’ souls, saving episcopal customs.

With these witnesses: Gregory, bishop of Dunkeld; Matthew, archdeacon of St Andrews; Master Andrew, archdeacon of Lothian; Geoffrey, abbot of Dunfermline; Alured, abbot of Stirling; William, abbot of Holyrood; Master Herbert; Master Abraham; Alexander the chaplain; Robert, son of Seulf of Perth; Stephen the clerk; Odo, brother of Archdeacon Matthew.
